scenario: Custom Outbound Connector returns object. Wonders return structure + Camunda binding.
question: Custom Connector return value?

options.text: md:233-291): the method can return any Java Object including a POJO, Map, String, number, or null. Jackson automatically serializes the returned object to JSON and the top-level fields become process variables. A POJO with fields paymentId and status would create two process variables of those names. The resultExpression task header allows FEEL-based transformation of the result before variable assignment. The resultVariable task header wraps the entire result under a single named variable. Null return means no variables are updated.

8 OutboundConnectorProvider API can return any Java object including POJOs, primitive types, String, or null. Jackson handles serialization of POJOs to JSON automatically based on field names and getter methods. A Map is one valid option, but a typed POJO is often preferred for type safety and better IDE support. The restriction to Map only applies to the legacy execute() API pattern, not the modern annotation-driven approach.

A custom serializer must always be implemented for Connector return values -- this overstates the requirement. The Connector SDK uses Jackson for automatic JSON serialization of return values. Standard Java types, POJOs with standard getters/setters, collections, and primitive wrappers all serialize correctly without any custom serializer. Custom Jackson annotations like @JsonProperty or @JsonIgnore can be added when field naming customization is needed. Only unusual types with complex serialization requirements -- such as custom date formats or polymorphic type handling -- might need a custom serializer configuration.

a.text: Incorrect. Custom Connector return: OutboundConnectorProvider + @Operation (canonical 8.8 SDK per connector-sdk.md:233-291): Verbatim example (:246-291): `java import io.camunda.connector.api.outbound.OutboundConnectorProvider; import io.camunda.connector.api.annotation.Operation; import io.camunda.connector.api.annotation.Variable; public class MyConnector implements OutboundConnectorProvider { @Operation(id = "operation1") public String operation1(@Variable(name = "input") String input) { ... } @Operation(id = "operation2") public MyOutput operation2(@Variable MyInput input) { ... } // complex type uses field names } Returns Object (any type) -- annotation-driven binding replaces the legacy execute(OutboundConnectorContext context) + context.bindVariables(...) pattern. Common return types: POJO: java public class PaymentResult { private String paymentId; private String status; private BigDecimal amount; // getters / setters } @Operation(id = "processPayment") public PaymentResult processPayment(@Variable PaymentInput input) { // ... do work PaymentResult result = new PaymentResult(); result.setPaymentId("pmt-123"); result.setStatus("completed"); result.setAmount(input.getAmount()); return result; } Map: java return Map.of( "paymentId", "pmt-123", "status", "completed", "amount", new BigDecimal("100") ); Single value: java return "simple-result"; Null: java return null; // no variables updated Serialisation: Jackson: POJO → JSON. Field names → variable names (top-level). Camel case typically (Java convention). Variable mapping: Default: result fields top-level in process scope. Customisation: resultExpression (per Set 16 Q17): FEEL transforms. resultVariable: name for whole result. Output Mapping: BPMN-level shaping. For Element Template: json { "label": "Result Variable", "type": "String", "binding": { "type": "zeebe:taskHeader", "key": "resultVariable" } } Modeler picks name. For Jackson configuration: java @JsonProperty("payment_id") private String paymentId; @JsonIgnore private String internalField; Customise serialisation. For dates / times: Use Java time types: LocalDate, Instant. Jackson serialises: ISO 8601. For decimals: Use BigDecimal: precision (per Set 13 Q48). Serialised: as number. For lists: java return new ListResult(List.of(item1, item2, item3)); Or: java return Map.of("items", List.of(...)); Lists become arrays in JSON. For nested objects: java public class Result { private Customer customer; private List items; // ... } Serialised as nested JSON. For null values: Null fields: typically included as null. Or excluded: configure Jackson @JsonInclude(NON_NULL). For testing: Mock context: return predefined. Verify result structure: in tests. For error vs success: Success: return result. Error: throw exception. BPMN error: throw specific exception type. For BPMN error throws: java if (failureCondition) { throw new ConnectorException("ERROR_CODE", "message"); } Verify exact exception type for BPMN errors per SDK. For metadata in result: Latency: include in result. Provider info: which service responded. Useful for: monitoring. For
